A coalition of longevity-focused companies is advocating for Montana to emerge as a leading hub for biohacking and experimental health treatments. This initiative aims to attract researchers and innovators in the field of longevity science, promoting an environment conducive to groundbreaking therapies and personalized health solutions.

The push comes amid growing interest in biohacking and the potential for novel interventions to extend healthspan. By positioning Montana as a center for these advancements, proponents believe it could catalyze significant clinical research and attract investment in longevity therapeutics. This could lead to a more robust ecosystem for startups and established firms alike, fostering collaboration and accelerating the translation of research into practical applications.

For professionals in the longevity sector, this development underscores the importance of supportive regulatory environments in advancing healthspan research. To delve deeper into the implications and details of this initiative, I encourage you to read the full article.

Source: [news.google.com](https://news.google.?oc=5)
